item.title: std::common-type in C++
item.description: std::common_type_t<T..> determines the common type from all give types within the angular brackets.

item.title: Trailing return type syntax for functions in C++
item.description: Trailing return type syntax is a function definition syntax where return type is specified at the end. We need to use auto keyword to use this syntax.

item.title: Type deductions for functions in C++
item.description: We can use auto with function return type to let compiler to infer type from return value.

item.title: Type deductions of objects using auto keyword in C++
item.description: Type deduction, also called as type inference, is a feature used by compiler to determining object types from the object’s initializer.

item.title: Creating type aliases in C++
item.description: C++ provides us using keyword to create type aliases to existing data types.

item.title: Reproducing builds using Spack Environments
item.description: A spack environment contains two main components: spack.yaml: gives an abstract details of specs and other configurations.

item.title: Environment Views
item.description: An environment view is a directory structure mirroring a typical Linux root directory containing directories such as /bin, /usr, etc.

item.title: Spack Environment
item.description: Spack Environment is a feature that allows us to group set of packages and easily use and manage them.
